title: drosophyllaceae
intro: The Drosophyllaceae is a family of flowering plants, specifically a group of small, succulent plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. This family consists of a single genus, Drosophyllum, which is characterized by its unique, carnivorous habit and adaptation to arid environments. The Drosophyllaceae family is relatively small, with only one species, Drosophyllum lusitanicum, widely recognized. Drosophyllaceae plants have evolved unique features such as sticky leaves and modified roots to capture insects and supplement their nutrient intake.
